(CNN) -- When the lights went out during the magic show, the Costa Concordia passengers watching thought it was part of the act. Then there was a scraping sound, the ship began to list to one side and a panicked evacuation began. One year ago, on January 13, 2012, the Costa Concordia struck rocks and turned on its side off the Italian island of Giglio, killing 32 people and shaking an industry that prefers to be known for fun in the sun and endless entertainment. Capt. Francesco Schettino may be indicted in the next few weeks on charges that include manslaughter and abandoning ship before his passengers, and other Concordia executives and crewmembers may also face trial. The ship is still in the water. Salvage experts hope to float the ship by the end of summer 2013 and eventually tow it to an undetermined port for salvage.

This is important because several hundred passengers who'd boarded

the Costa Concordia for a relaxing vacation were instead forced to face a terrifying ordeal, with many losing their lives in the process. As the captain and crew members face potential charges for their actions during the disaster, the families of the victims and survivors anxiously await justice.

In the end, the investigation into the tragic incident leads to major changes in the cruise industry, with new safety regulations implemented to prevent a similar catastrophe from happening again. Captain Schettino and several other crew members are eventually found guilty of various charges, including manslaughter and abandoning ship. They face long prison sentences, and their cases serve as a reminder of the responsibilities that come with being in charge of a ship and the lives of those on board.

The Costa Concordia is finally refloated and towed to a port where it is dismantled for scrap, marking the end of the vessel's tumultuous story. The victim's families and survivors are awarded compensation for their losses and trauma, but the emotional scars left by the disaster linger. The cruise industry makes a concerted effort to rebuild its reputation, emphasizing safety and responsible practices to regain the trust of the public.

Throughout all of this, the world remembers the Costa Concordia tragedy as a somber reminder of the importance of following safety procedures and valuing human life above all else. As time passes, the events of that fateful day continue to serve as a lesson on the weight of responsibility that comes with leading others, both on the open seas and beyond.

The term "to give serious consideration" becomes "antamaan vakavaa harkintaa," implying that the Commission is putting significant thought and effort into the matter. "To the possibility of concluding bilateral trade agreements" is expressed as "mahdollisuudelle tehdä kahdenvälisiä kauppasopimuksia." Tip: Incorrect CBD S file association errors can be a symptom of other underlying issues within your Windows operating system.

Incorrect CBD S file association errors can occur when your computer's operating system doesn't properly associate a file type with a specific program or application. This can lead to difficulties when trying to open a file, as the system may not know which program to use, or it may try to open the file with an incompatible program. These errors can be indicative of other underlying issues within your Windows operating system that require resolution.

There are several steps you can take to address incorrect CBD S file association errors and their related issues:

Article:

The race is on for the final spots on stage for the first Republican debate. 
  
 With the highly-anticipated showdown less than two weeks away, candidates with low polling numbers are in a high-stakes scramble to qualify for an event that represents their best shot at breaking out. 
  
 Fox News is capping the Aug. 6 debate in Cleveland at 10 candidates based on five as-of-yet unspecified national polls released by 5 p.m. on Aug. 4. 
  
 ADVERTISEMENT 
  
 Based on five polls used by RealClearPolitics (RCP), eight candidates look like locks to make the stage, while the race for the final two slots is headed for a controversial photo finish. For the candidates currently ranked between ninth place and 14th place, the polling differential is negligible. 
  
 “It’s a roll of the dice,” said Monmouth University polling director Patrick Murray. “It’s going to come down to the vagaries of how independent pollsters round off their results — we’re talking tenths of decimal points. It could come down to the five or six people who didn’t pick up their phones for a national survey and the five or six people who did.” 
  
 New Jersey Gov. Chris Christie, Ohio Gov. John Kasich, former Texas Gov. Rick Perry James (Rick) Richard PerryTexas New Members 2019 Senate should reject Trump’s radical nominee to key energy panel Perry: We shouldn't let Russia use energy as a weapon MORE, businesswoman Carly Fiorina, former Pennsylvania Sen. Rick Santorum and Louisiana Gov. Bobby Jindal are separated by only 1.5 percentage points, according to the RCP national average at the end of last week. 
  
 Christie looks to be in the best shape of the bunch, sitting in 9th place with 2.8 percent support. Perry and Kasich are tied for 10th with 1.8 percent support, followed by Fiorina and Santorum at 1.4 percent, and Jindal at 1.2. (By contrast, the lowest of the top eight, Sen. Ted Cruz (R-Texas), has 5.4 percent support; the highest, Donald Trump, has 18.2 percent.) 
  
 Getting left out of the first debate would be a significant blow to all of their prospects. 
  
 “It’s extremely important to be out there,” said Republican strategist Ron Bonjean. “If you’re not there, it’s out-of-sight-out-of-mind for voters, and just as importantly right now, for your fundraising efforts.” 
  
 For Christie and Santorum, missing the debate would put a spotlight on how far their stars have fallen. 
  
 Christie was once flying high in the polls. As early as last year he was considered by many a favorite to win the nomination. And Santorum is the most recent winner of the Iowa caucuses, having emerged in 2012 as the primary challenger to eventual nominee Mitt Romney. 
  
 Meanwhile, Kasich risks being frozen out of a debate that will take place in a state where he’s the sitting governor. For Perry, it would mean a missed opportunity for redemption after a mortifying brain-freeze on the debate stage essentially ended his presidential run in 2012. 
  
 A spot on the debate stage could legitimize Fiorina’s outsider bid while allowing her to showcase her much-lauded skills as a communicator. And some Republicans believe Jindal’s firebrand conservatism could catch on in Iowa, if only he could get the exposure. 
  
 It’s setting up as a dogfight among the campaigns to boost their standing nationally ahead of the debates. It means weighing the return on investment of spending early in an attempt to make the debate, versus mapping out a longer-term strategy. 
  
 “We’ve never seen anything like this,” said GOP strategist David Payne. “These candidates who are not polling well are forced to spend early money for national ad buys, while the earned media pathway is complicated by Donald Trump, who is costing every other candidate a chance to get in front of the camera. It’s a challenge.” 
  
 The campaign strategies for how the candidates plan to address that challenge are coming into view. 
  
 Christie’s campaign is betting big that a national advertising push can shore up his standing. This week, the campaign put $250,000 behind ads that will run on Fox News. The New Jersey governor is also getting an assist from a supporting super-PAC, which last week put more than $1 million into ads that will run in the Northeast. 
  
 Perry is hitching his train to Donald Trump. He has emerged as the most vocal opponent of the businessman and reality TV star, who is riding a media frenzy to the top of the polls. Perry could get a lift from Trump’s media coattails. 
  
 In addition, the former Texas governor is getting help from the well-funded super-PACs backing his bid. The Opportunity and Freedom PAC has booked nearly $1 million in national ads on conservative TV and radio outlets ahead of the debate. 
  
 Austin Barbour, who runs the PAC, noted that Perry tied for seventh in last week’s ABC News-Washington Post poll. “My hope is that we’ll see a couple more polls like that because of this earned media he’s getting off Trump, the speeches he’s giving and what we’ve been doing with paid media.” 
  
 Meanwhile, Santorum said at an event in Washington earlier this month that he’d ramp up his media appearances before the debate, and he’s following through. On Wednesday, Santorum did two Fox News hits, appeared on CNN and a Bloomberg online show and finished the day sparring with liberal host Rachel Maddow on MSNBC. 
  
 Kasich’s political team says their strategy is to focus on New Hampshire rather than on resources meant to raise his profile nationally. But the Ohio governor’s political team spent significantly on TV ads ahead of his launch and will hope his late entrance into the race is perfectly timed to give him a polling boost heading into August. 
  
 Predictably, the complaining over Fox News’s debate standards has picked up in recent days. 
  
 Sen. Lindsey Graham Lindsey Olin GrahamSunday shows preview: New members preview agendas after Democratic House takeover Trump set to have close ally Graham in powerful chairmanship On The Money: Why the tax law failed to save the GOP majority | Grassley opts for Finance gavel, setting Graham up for Judiciary | Trump says China eager for trade deal | Facebook reeling after damning NYT report MORE (R-S.C.), who is barely registering in national polls, said on MSNBC last week that the parameters “suck,” while an adviser to Jindal’s campaign wrote an op-ed in The Wall Street Journal arguing that every Republican running for president deserves a prime-time slot. 
  
 “When did we start fearing debates? And if we do fear debates, what business do we have trying to win elections,” wrote Curt Anderson. “The plan to limit the participants in these debates is ridiculous in almost every respect.” 
  
 The Louisiana governor has temporarily suspended his campaign to deal with a shooting at a movie theater in Lafayette. 
  
 Fox News is holding a one-hour forum for the candidates who don’t qualify for the main event earlier in the day. 
  
 “That secondary forum will have a lot of qualified, highly knowledgeable and articulate folks,” said Dave Catalfamo, a spokesperson for former New York Gov. George Pataki, who also barely registers in the polls. “It might end up being more substantive because the candidates won’t have to respond to whatever idiotic thing Donald Trump says.” 
  
 But most Republicans view it as a second-tier gathering nonetheless. 
  
 “If you’re not in that top 10, you’re not going to be taken as seriously,” said Bonjean. “You risk getting left behind." ||||| Fox News is opening its 5 p.m. debate to all the announced Republican candidates who fail to make the cut for the Aug. 6 prime-time event, removing a requirement that participants reach at least 1 percent in polling. 
  
 The change amounts to an insurance policy for candidates who were in danger of being disqualified from the vital first debate based on low polls – Carly Fiorina, former New York Gov. George Pataki and Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.). 
  
 Story Continued Below 
  
 The announcement by Michael Clemente, Fox News Executive Vice President, News, means that all 16 announced candidates will qualify for Cleveland — either the 5 p.m. undercard, or the 9 p.m. main event. 
  
 The 9 p.m. debate will include the 10 candidates with the highest average in national polls, as determined by Fox News. The 5 p.m. forum will now include all the rest. 
  
 According to a POLITICO analysis of the latest national polling, the prime-time participants today would be Donald Trump, Jeb Bush, Wisconsin Gov. Scott Walker, Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.), Sen. Ted Cruz (R-Tex.), Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.), Mike Huckabee, Ben Carson, New Jersey Gov. Chris Christie and Rick Perry. 
  
 The next three, who would currently be relegated to 5 p.m., are Ohio Gov. John Kasich, Rick Santorum and Louisiana Gov. Bobby Jindal. They are followed by Fiorina, Pataki and Graham. 
  
 All of the candidates have been getting extensive Fox airtime. Here’s a tally of the total combined Fox News Channel and Fox Business Network appearances — several for an hour — by each of the hopefuls since their official campaign launches: 
  
 1) Paul, 35 … 2) Huckabee, 31 … 3) Trump, 30 … 4) Perry, 24 … 5-6) Fiorina and Jindal, 20 each … 7) Cruz, 17 … 8) Santorum, 16 … 9) Rubio, 14 … 10-11) Carson and Graham, 12 each … 12-13) Kasich and Pataki, 11 each … 14) Christie, 7 … 15) Walker, 4 … 16) Bush, 3. 
  
 “Due to the overwhelming interest in the FOX News Facebook Debate Event Night on August 6th and in a concerted effort to include and accommodate the now 16 Republican candidate field — the largest in modern political history — FOX News is expanding participation in the 5 PM/ET debate to all declared candidates whose names are consistently being offered to respondents in major national polls, as recognized by Fox News,” said Clemente in a statement. 
  
 “Although we are relaxing one component of our entry criteria – the requirement that candidates must score 1% or higher in an average of five most recent national polls – all other components of the criteria remain in effect for the 5 PM/ET debate. Participants must meet all U.S. Constitutional requirements; must announce and register a formal campaign for president; and must file all necessary paperwork with the Federal Election Commission (FEC), including financial disclosure. 
  
 “As for the 9 PM/ET debate, all components of the original criteria remain unchanged – including the requirement that participants must place in the top 10 of an average of the five most recent national polls, as recognized by FOX News, leading up to August 4th at 5PM/ET. Such polling must be conducted by major, nationally recognized organizations that use standard methodological techniques. 
  
 “Everyone included in these debates has a chance to be President of the United States and we look forward to showcasing all of the candidates in the first primary event of the 2016 election season.” 
  
 The 5 p.m. debate, which will last an hour, will be moderated by Bill Hemmer and Martha MacCallum. The 9 p.m. debate will run about 90 minutes (two hours with commercial breaks and introductions), and be moderated by Bret Baier, Megyn Kelly and Chris Wallace. 
  
 Follow @politico |||||

The first Republican debate is less than two weeks away and the race is on for the final spots on stage, especially for those candidates with low polling numbers. Fox News is capping the August 6 debate at 10 candidates and based on five polls by RealClearPolitics, eight candidates look like they will make the stage while the others will have a photo finish. This event represents their best chance to break out, therefore it's creating a high-stakes scramble to qualify for those with low polling numbers.

Information:  - Africa is the world's second-largest and second-most-populous continent. Improvisation, within the context of performing arts, is a very spontaneous performance without specific or scripted preparation. Jazz originated in New Orleans as a fusion of African and European music and played a significant part in wider cultural changes in this period, and its influence on pop culture continued long afterwards. The Jazz Age is often referred to in conjunction with the Roaring Twenties.  - A music genre is a conventional category that identifies some pieces of music as belonging to a shared tradition or set of conventions. It is to be distinguished from "musical form" and "musical style", although in practice these terms are sometimes used interchangeably. Recently, academics have argued that categorizing music by genre is inaccurate and outdated.  - Polyrhythm is the simultaneous use of two or more conflicting rhythms, that are not readily perceived as deriving from one another, or as simple manifestations of the same meter. The rhythmic conflict may be the basis of an entire piece of music (cross-rhythm), or a momentary disruption. Polyrhythms can be distinguished from irrational rhythms, which can occur within the context of a single part; polyrhythms require at least two rhythms to be played concurrently, one of which is typically an irrational rhythm.  - Jazz is a music genre that originated amongst African Americans in New Orleans, United States, in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. Since the 1920s jazz age, jazz has become recognized as a major form of musical expression. It emerged in the form of independent traditional and popular musical styles, all linked by the common bonds of African American and European American musical parentage with a performance orientation. Jazz is characterized by swing and blue notes, call and response vocals, polyrhythms and improvisation. Jazz has roots in West African cultural and musical expression, and in African-American music traditions including blues and ragtime, as well as European military band music. Although the foundation of jazz is deeply rooted within the Black experience of the United States, different cultures have contributed their own experience and styles to the art form as well. Intellectuals around the world have hailed jazz as "one of America's original art forms".  - Blues is a genre and musical form originated by African Americans in the Deep South of the United States around the end of the 19th century. The genre developed from roots in African musical traditions, African-American work songs and European-American folk music. Blues incorporated spirituals, work songs, field hollers, shouts, chants, and rhymed simple narrative ballads. The blues form, ubiquitous in jazz, rhythm and blues and rock and roll, is characterized by the call-and-response pattern, the blues scale and specific chord progressions, of which the twelve-bar blues is the most common. Blue notes (or "worried notes"), usually thirds or fifths flattened in pitch, are also an essential part of the sound. Blues shuffles or walking bass reinforce the trance-like rhythm and form a repetitive effect known as the groove.  - New Orleans (or ) is a major United States port and the largest city and metropolitan area in the state of Louisiana. The population of the city was 343,829 as of the 2010 U.S. Census. The New Orleans metropolitan area (New OrleansMetairieKenner Metropolitan Statistical Area) had a population of 1,167,764 in 2010 and was the 46th largest in the United States. The New OrleansMetairieBogalusa Combined Statistical Area, a larger trading area, had a 2010 population of 1,452,502.  - Popular music is music with wide appeal that is typically distributed to large audiences through the music industry. Translate to English

English:

During our presidency, we aim to develop evaluation systems for justice and home affairs based on the European Commission's communication.

As the chair of the presidency, our main focus will be on enhancing cooperation and collaboration among the European Union member states in the field of justice and home affairs. This is in line with the priorities of the European Commission's communication, which emphasizes improving the mutual understanding, trust, and overall effectiveness of the justice and home affairs systems within the EU.

To achieve this goal, we will work closely with all EU member states, European Union institutions, and relevant stakeholders to create an effective, transparent, and robust evaluation mechanism for justice and home affairs. This evaluation system will be designed to identify best practices, detect weaknesses, and address emerging challenges in the field.

In order to develop this evaluation system, our presidency has identified several key areas that we will focus on:

1. Strengthening the rule of law: We will prioritize efforts to maintain and enhance the rule of law within the European Union, including exploring ways to better monitor and assess the state of rule of law in member states. This will involve working with relevant stakeholders and institutions to create harmonized criteria, indicators, and mechanisms for evaluation.

2. Enhancing judicial cooperation: We aim to improve the efficiency of cross-border cooperation in judicial matters among EU member states, including exploring ways to streamline the exchange of information and increase the use of digital tools. Additionally, we will promote the adoption of integrated case management systems for better communication and information sharing among judicial authorities.

3. Improving the fight against organized crime and terrorism: We will promote the development of common strategies, policies, and legislative frameworks to tackle organized crime and terrorism more effectively. This will involve strengthening cooperation with Europol, Eurojust, and other relevant agencies, as well as working together with member states to improve the exchange of information, intelligence, and best practices.

4. Enhancing migration and asylum policies: We will focus on promoting a common European approach to migration and asylum policies, including the development of coherent and effective evaluation mechanisms. These mechanisms will consider the integration of migrants into society, the respect for human rights, and cooperation with third countries to address the root causes of migration.

5. Promoting civil protection and crisis management: We will work to enhance the EU’s capacity to respond to crises and emergencies, including the development of an integrated assessment framework for civil protection and crisis management. This framework will enable member states and EU institutions to evaluate and monitor their preparedness and response capabilities effectively, ensuring that the EU remains resilient in the face of future challenges.

In conclusion, our presidency will focus on developing comprehensive evaluation systems for justice and home affairs that closely align with the European Commission's communication. By working together with all EU member states, institutions, and stakeholders, we strive to build a stronger, safer, and more united European Union for all citizens.

Article:

NEW ORLEANS -- Ex-House Speaker Newt Gingrich is spending a day among conservative activists acting like a WH'12 contender, hosting events and offering a packed ballroom a vision for the GOP he may one day lead. 
  
 Keynoting the Southern Republican Leadership Conference Thursday night, Gingrich used his nearly hour-long address to alternatively slam Pres. Obama while laying out his own vision for the future of the GOP. 
  
 "What the left wants to do is to say we're the 'Party of No,'" Gingrich said. "I think we should decide we're going to be the party of yes." 
  
 Gingrich urged SRLC attendees to advocate for common GOP principles that closely mirror the '94 Contract with America. He said the GOP supports balanced budgets, tight controls on spending and what he called "the right kind" of health care reform. "There are many things that we can say yes to," he said. "Our first answer should be, 'Let me tell you what I'm for,' and the answer should be first principles." 
  
 The long-time leader of the GOP's intellectual wing still offers pointed barbs at an admin he sees as "the most radical" in the nation's history. "This is going to be, in economic terms, the worst administration since Herbert Hoover," he said. 
  
 He offered bullets on his resume intermittently throughout his speech, reminding activists of his central role in crafting the GOP's return to the majority for the first time in 40 years -- and his role in re-electing the first GOP majority in 64 years. 
  
 His experience will play a role in the GOP's strategy to roll back the health care overhaul. But he spent a chunk of the time discussing the conversation he wants to have with conservatives and Americans over the next three years. 
  
 "I used to be Speaker of the House and I actually understand the legislative process," he said. The "repeal and replace" message, he noted, might not succeed in '11 -- as Dems have said -- but he said that with a GOP president and a GOP Congress in '13, they will repeal all the "radical" legislation Obama has gotten through. 
  
 And given the reaction he's received, it's clear Gingrich will be a major player. He retains an almost cult following among conservative activists. At the SRLC, as he did earlier this year at the Conservative Political Action Committee, he entered from the back of the hall to the '80s power ballad "Eye of the Tiger" instead of from behind the podium, reveling in the crush of supporters. 
  
 But major speeches to conservative activists aren't Gingrich's only forays into the WH'12 water. Before delivering his keynote address, Gingrich hosted his fifth campaign-style town hall event at a nearby hotel. He also met with a group of small business owners to hear their concerns over a sagging economy. 
  
 He has considered running for the WH before, but demurred. This time, Gingrich told an audience member he believes he and his wife will decide whether to make a WH bid in '11. "That'll [be up to] God and the American people," he said. 
  
 If he decides to run, he'll have a fan base. After one event, local Tea Party leader Evan Spiceland said that Gingrich has always been the smartest of the GOP field, and that the rest have to sell themselves to activists.  NEW ORLEANS -- Newt Gingrich made a rock star's entrance at the Southern Republican Leadership Conference Thursday night, entering to a standing ovation as the song "Eye of the Tiger" blared over the speakers and lights shined out over the crowd. 
  
 When Gingrich eventually got to the podium, he delivered a self-assured address peppered with historical allusions. Democrats in Washington, he said, had put together a "perfect unrepresentative left-wing machine dedicated to a secular socialist future." 
  
 Mr. Obama is "the most radical president in American history," Gingrich said. "He has said, 'I run a machine, I own Washington, and there is nothing you can do about it.'" 
  
 "What we need is a president, not an athlete," Gingrich said during a question and answer period after his speech. He added: "Shooting three point shots may be clever, but it doesn't put anybody to work." 
  
 Gingrich discussed passage of the health care bill, saying the "decisive" election of Sen. Scott Brown sent a message that Democrats decided to ignore in order to "ram through" the bill against the wishes of the American people. 
  
 "The longer Obama talks the less the American people believe him," Gingrich said, citing the decline in poll numbers for the health care bill as the president kept trying to sell it to the public. 
  
 Gingrich said that when Republicans take back the House and Senate in the midterm elections they should "refuse to fund" the administration's proposals, drawing huge applause from the crowd. 
  
 Add a Republican president, he said, and the goal should be to "repeal every radical bill" passed by the current administration. 
  
 Gingrich, a potential 2012 Republican presidential candidate, stressed his conception of the current administration as part of "secular, socialist machine." 
  
 "On every front," he said, "they're increasing government" and trying to "micromanage our lives," raise taxes, increase government power and strip citizens of their power. 
  
 "This is a fundamental fight over the core definition of America," Gingrich said. He told the crowd they should be talking about culture, not politics. "The more we make this a choice about the nature of America, the weaker they are," he said. 
  
 Gingrich told a story about the Polish people when they were resisting communism, who he said came up with the slogan, "two plus two equals four," taking it from the writings of Albert Camus and George Orwell. He said the slogan grew from the fact that the authorities can't handle the truth, tying it to what he suggested were nonsensical numbers offered by Democrats about the health care bill. 
  
 "There are more of us than there are of the government," the former House speaker said, midway through his story about Polish resistance to communism. "So why are we afraid of them?" 
  
 Asked if he would run for president, he said he would make a decision in February 2011. He said for the moment it was important for Republicans to "get out and campaign this year to win this year's election." 
  
 The left, Gingrich said, wants "to be dishonest about who we are" and cast Republicans as "the party of no." 
  
 He said the GOP needs to be the party of yes -- by saying yes to a balanced budget through controlled spending, more jobs through tax cuts, reforming government, stopping people from stealing from Medicaid and Medicare, spending more on getting soldiers better equipment, and "the right kind of health reform" and energy plan. 
  
 "My point is, there are many things that we can say yes to, and if we would discipline ourselves to start off every conversation with...'let me tell you what I'm for,'" Gingrich said, Republicans will be on the right track. 
  
 Gingrich predicted that "this administration is going to be a catastrophic failure," deeming it the worst since Herbert Hoover from an economic perspective. He pushed for small business tax cuts over government spending, 
  
 "Quite frankly, I'm tired of finding new ways to help people who aren't working, I want to find new ways to help people who are working," he said. 
  
 Gingrich said Americans will choose Republicans in the coming elections if Republicans can frame the discussion between what they offer and what is offered by Democrats. He said President Obama had helped set the stage for a comeback with his "incompetence." 
  
 The debate should be framed, he said, not as "Obama vs. Anti-Obama, but America vs. a secular socialist machine."

However, when you look at things closer to your face, the blinking slows down. This reduces the amount of tears and causes discomfort in your eyes. Write an article based on this summary:

Tell yourself that being a woman is a mindset. Read coming-of-age books by and about women. Learn appropriate social skills and behaviors. Act with confidence. Learn about the world. Take part in a coming-of-age ritual. Make goals for yourself and live up to them.

Article:

Title: Embracing Womanhood: The Power of Mindset and Personal Growth

Introduction
Embracing womanhood requires much more than just growing older or reaching specific milestones. Womanhood is a mindset, and it can encompass a range of experiences, personal growth, and self-awareness. Empowered women are active participants in their life journey, and they continuously push the boundaries of what it means to be a woman in today's society. In this article, we will explore different ways to nurture a woman's mindset, personal growth, and overall well-being.

1. Being a Woman is a Mindset
The first step in embracing your womanhood is to understand that being a woman is a mindset. Recognize that this mindset encompasses both physical and emotional traits, and it calls for openness, courage, and resilience in the face of life's challenges. Tell yourself that being a woman means embracing these qualities, and be proud of who you are.

2. Read Coming-of-Age Books by and about Women
Reading coming-of-age books can provide valuable insights into the lives and experiences of other women. These books tell stories of personal growth, identity, and self-discovery, and they can teach us essential lessons that help us navigate our own paths to womanhood. So, pick up a few books, immerse yourself in the lives of inspiring women, and learn from their struggles and triumphs.

3. Learn Appropriate Social Skills and Behaviors
Developing social skills and displaying appropriate behavior are essential parts of embracing womanhood. As a woman, understanding the nuances of communication, empathy, and effective problem-solving can enhance your relationships and improve your overall well-being. Make it a point to learn and practice these skills, as they will shape the way you interact with people and the world around you.

4. Act with Confidence
A major aspect of embracing womanhood involves acting with confidence. Confidence allows you to express yourself authentically, make decisions in line with your values, and feel secure in your identity. To cultivate confidence, consider surrounding yourself with supportive individuals, take advantage of opportunities to challenge yourself, and be unapologetically you.

5. Learn About the World
Expanding your knowledge of the world around you can have a profound impact on your personal growth and understanding of yourself as a woman. Travel, engage with diverse communities, or educate yourself on global issues to build a stronger connection with the world and develop a more comprehensive view of your own place within it.

6. Take Part in a Coming-of-Age Ritual
Partaking in a coming-of-age ritual can provide a symbolic transition into womanhood. Consider creating your own ritual or engage in cultural or religious practices that resonate with you. Coming-of-age rituals can serve as a reminder of your personal growth and provide an opportunity to reflect on your journey thus far.

7. Make Goals for Yourself and Live Up to Them
Setting and working towards personal goals is an essential part of embracing your womanhood. Reflect on your values and aspirations, and identify goals that will help you develop personally, professionally, and emotionally. Ensure that your goals align with your overarching vision of yourself as a woman, and commit to taking small steps each day to bring you closer to realizing these ambitions.

Conclusion
Embracing womanhood necessitates a mindset that encompasses personal growth, self-awareness, and a dedication to experiencing life to the fullest. By reading about the experiences of other women, honing social skills, acting with confidence, learning about the world, taking part in coming-of-age rituals, and setting personal goals, you can cultivate an empowered sense of womanhood that will shape your life for years to come.

Please answer the following question: Question: "According to Pope John XXIII, one cannot ascend to a cardinal position without first holding what office?"  Context: "At various times, there have been cardinals who had only received first tonsure and minor orders but not yet been ordained as deacons or priests. Though clerics, they were inaccurately called "lay cardinals" and were permitted to marry. Teodolfo Mertel was among the last of the lay cardinals. When he died in 1899 he was the last surviving cardinal who was not at least ordained a priest. With the revision of the Code of Canon Law promulgated in 1917 by Pope Benedict XV, only those who are already priests or bishops may be appointed cardinals. Since the time of Pope John XXIII a priest who is appointed a cardinal must be consecrated a bishop, unless he obtains a dispensation."  Answer:
Answer:

According to Pope John XXIII, one cannot ascend to a cardinal position without first holding the office of a bishop. This requirement was established to ensure that those appointed as cardinals have the necessary ecclesiastical experience, knowledge, and authority to hold such a significant position within the Catholic Church.

The context provided gives a historical background of cardinals appointed in various times. In the past, there were cases where individuals who had only received first tonsure and minor orders, but were not yet ordained as deacons or priests, were appointed as cardinals. These clerics were inaccurately referred to as "lay cardinals," and they were even allowed to marry. Teodolfo Mertel, who died in 1899, was one of the last lay cardinals, marking the end of an era when cardinals were not necessarily ordained priests.

The revision of the Code of Canon Law in 1917, promulgated by Pope Benedict XV, brought about a significant change in the criteria for appointing cardinals. The revised law stated that only those who are already priests or bishops may be appointed as cardinals. This notable shift in policy aimed to ensure that cardinals would have the appropriate clerical background and qualifications to effectively carry out their responsibilities within the Church.

Continuing this progression, Pope John XXIII further refined the requirements for becoming a cardinal. He determined that a priest who is appointed a cardinal must be consecrated a bishop unless a dispensation, or exemption, is granted. This decree reinforces the importance of having an experienced and authoritative figure in the cardinal position, as bishops possess the highest order of the sacrament of Holy Orders and are entrusted with the administration of the sacraments and the governance of the Church.

In conclusion, Pope John XXIII made it a requirement for a priest to be consecrated as a bishop first before ascending to a cardinal position, unless a dispensation is obtained. This rule highlights the importance of having experienced, knowledgeable, and authoritative individuals in the cardinalate to ensure that the complex duties and responsibilities of the position are effectively carried out. The historical progression, from having "lay cardinals" to requiring cardinals to be bishops, demonstrates the evolving nature of Catholic Church leadership and the criteria associated with its highest-ranking officials.

How to calculate amount of unemployment in ohio 
Meet the criteria for unemployment insurance. 
There are a certain number of factors that determine whether or not you are eligible for unemployment compensation. If the following applies to you, then you are considered unemployed and are eligible for unemployment insurance and benefits.

First and foremost, you must be either partially or totally unemployed through no fault of your own. This means that if you have been let go from your job, it must have been due to lack of work or business closure, rather than for misconduct or quitting voluntarily. In the case of partial unemployment, your work hours must have significantly reduced due to circumstances beyond your control.

Additionally, you must have a qualifying base period of employment to establish eligibility. In Ohio, the base period is determined by looking at the first four out of the last five completed calendar quarters before the start of your unemployment claim. For instance, if you file your claim in July 2021, your base period would comprise of the four quarters between April 2020 and March 2021. Within this base period, you must have worked in at least two quarters and earned a minimum pre-determined amount, which is subject to adjustment each year based on average weekly wages in the state.

Moreover, you must be able and available to work during the entire period of your unemployment. This means meeting the physical and mental requirements essential for your line of work, as well as being prepared to accept suitable job offers. Suitability is typically determined by factors such as prior skills and experience, previous wage level, commuting distance, and the working environment. It is also crucial to actively seek employment, making genuine efforts to find a new job by conducting job searches, submitting applications, attending job interviews, and networking with potential employers.

Furthermore, you may be required to participate in reemployment services provided by the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS). These services aim to assist claimants in re-entering the workforce, offering resources such as job search assistance, skills assessment, training, and workshops. Failure to participate in these services without good cause may result in disqualification from receiving unemployment benefits.

To calculate the amount of unemployment compensation you are entitled to receive, Ohio utilizes a formula based on your earnings during the base period. A weekly benefit is calculated by taking the average of your earnings in the two highest-earning quarters of your base period, then dividing that amount by 26. The maximum amount of benefits that can be collected each week is currently capped at around 50% of the state's average weekly wage. Once your weekly benefit is determined, you can receive payments for a maximum of 26 weeks within a 12-month benefit period, provided that you continue to meet the eligibility requirements.

In conclusion, calculating the amount of unemployment in Ohio depends on meeting specific eligibility criteria, such as job separation through no fault of your own, sufficient earnings and employment within a base period, being able and available to work, and participating in reemployment services. Once these qualifications are met, a calculation based on your earnings during the base period determines the weekly benefit amount and the duration of the compensation.

Una din slăbiciunile etichetei este faptul că consumatorii nu sunt conştienţi de aceasta, iar în această privinţă sunt de acord cu dl Wijkman şi, prin urmare, aceasta nu este deosebit de atractivă nici pentru producători.

Translate to English

One of the weaknesses of the labeling system is that consumers are not aware of it, and in this regard, I agree with Mr. Wijkman, and therefore, it is not particularly attractive for manufacturers either.

The labeling system is an essential element in the commercialization of products, as it aims to inform consumers about the characteristics, qualities, and origin of a product. However, its effectiveness can be significantly impeded by various factors. One such factor is the lack of awareness among consumers regarding the existence of these labeling systems. This lack of awareness can negatively affect both consumers and producers.

Consumer awareness plays a vital role in the success of a labeling system. If consumers are unaware of the labels, they will not be able to make informed decisions when purchasing products. Consequently, the intended purpose of the labeling system in promoting transparency in the market and facilitating consumer choice is compromised. The lack of awareness of the labeling system can be attributed to numerous sources, including insufficient marketing and promotion efforts, complex labeling information, and a scarcity of educational programs aimed at increasing consumer knowledge.

For manufacturers and producers, an ineffective labeling system creates a scenario where the investment made in obtaining certifications, conducting tests, and implementing necessary measures to be included in a labeling system does not yield the expected benefits. This lack of incentives for manufacturers may result in a reduction in the number of companies willing to apply and adhere to environmentally friendly or socially responsible principles. Consequently, this might lead to a decline in overall quality and sustainability in the market.

Given these repercussions, it is essential to address the issue of consumer awareness regarding the labeling system to ensure its effectiveness for all stakeholders involved. There are multiple strategies that can be employed to improve consumer awareness of the labeling system:

1. Improving marketing and promotional efforts: This includes investing in advertising, social media campaigns, and public relations activities that aim to increase the visibility and understanding of the labeling system in the eyes of consumers.

2. Simplifying labeling information: The complexity of labeling systems can often overwhelm consumers and create difficulties in understanding the meaning and implications of various labels. Streamlining and simplifying the labeling information can enhance consumers' understanding and awareness.

3. Implementing educational programs: Developing and incorporating educational programs and initiatives that focus on the importance of label information and how to interpret the data can significantly boost the overall awareness of consumers.

4. Collaboration with retailers: Working closely with retailers can ensure that labeling information is prominently displayed and easily accessible for consumers. Retailers can also be trained to provide additional information on the labeling system to their customers.

By adopting these strategies and maintaining an ongoing commitment to improving consumer awareness, the weaknesses of the labeling system can be mitigated, and it can serve as an influential tool for both consumers and manufacturers.

Reuters Holdings PLC said Michael Reupke resigned as general manager to pursue unspecified interests, a move the news organization termed an "amicable separation." Mr. Reupke, 52 years old and a 27-year Reuters veteran, had been the information-services company's general manager for only six months. His appointment to that post, which has senior administrative, staff and policy responsibilities, followed a several-year tenure as Reuters's editor in chief. A Primeval Passage on Caddo Lake in East Texas
EVERY visitor to Caddo Lake on the Texas-Louisiana border seems to come away with a profoundly personal impression. Anglers prize Caddo, the only naturally formed lake in Texas, for its trophy bass; canoeists rate its twisting, interlocking bayous among the most challenging to navigate.

Environmentalists cherish its 26,000 acres as a rare, if not singular, wetlands environment. Caddo is more of a large bayou, composed of many smaller waterways. It is home to over 200 species of birds; hundreds of kinds of mammals, reptiles and fish; and countless plants, most prominently the towering baldcypress  some as old as 400 years  that erupt from its surface like limbs of drowned giants.

But what struck me about Caddo Lake the first time I saw it was the powerful suggestion of the supernatural that it evoked. Honestly, it’s kind of a creepy place.

Spectral shapes shift in the mist that rises from its cilantro-colored surface; ghost stories seem to ooze from its forested shores. Spanish moss dangles like serpents from cypress branches; spooky tales lurk behind the wary eyes of egrets and rabbits.

Natural beauty by itself can stop you dead in your tracks, but Caddo also works on the primal circuits. It sticks with you.

That, perhaps, is why my wife, Ann, and I return every few years to the lake, which sits about 15 miles west of Shreveport, La.: to make sure that what we saw the first time really existed; and maybe to figure it out, learn from it, even befriend it.

For John Winn, 48, one of the lake’s most prominent guides, Caddo has been a lifelong companion. “In a way, this lake raised me,” he said, piloting his shallow-draft Go-Devil boat through the cypresses, which in early spring were equal parts dead graying foliage and new green growth. “Most everything important I’ve ever done has been on this lake.”

Among Caddo’s many mysteries is its genesis. The lake was named for the Caddo Indians, who settled in the area in the early 1600s. There are two theories of its creation: The Caddo Indians held that it was formed by an earthquake and flooding; other, more recent histories say it was the result of the Great Raft  a huge logjam in the Red River, which in the early 1800s forced enough water into Big Cypress Bayou to create the lake.

At the same time, the bayou became deep and wide enough to handle big steamships, which began heading up the Mississippi from New Orleans, then northeastward on the Red River and all the way to the hamlet of Jefferson, 15 miles west of the lake.

As the 19th century progressed, Jefferson became Texas’s second-largest port after Galveston, a jumping-off point for goods headed for the fast-developing West. The town boomed, and the lake was a favored hideout for criminal elements drawn to all the new money  leading to the claim that, during this era, there was a murder a day in the area. Maybe it is the ghosts of these murderers and thieves that give the lake such a preternatural spookiness: what happened at Caddo, stayed in Caddo.

Sometimes, the legends around Caddo cross over into the popular imagination.

“I get requests all the time for Bigfoot tours,” Mr. Winn said, stroking at a gray-and-black beard. “Fine. But I’ve been out here all my life. I know places out here that the fishes don’t know about. I’ve never seen Bigfoot.”

Mr. Winn turned the Go-Devil down one of the lake’s 42 mapped and marked “boat roads” in search of the animals that definitely inhabit the lake. A spring cold snap seemed to be keeping the alligators out of sight, but we saw plenty of turtles and frogs, hawks and vultures soaring overhead.

The most interesting thing we saw was a beaver dam  a good five feet high and half again as wide, banked up against a cypress tree  looking every bit as solid as an Allied bunker. “These beavers back in here will get to be 70 pounds or so, and it’s all muscle,” Mr. Winn said. “There’s not much that can stand up to one except maybe for a gator, and even they find them too tough to eat.”

Like a lot of ecological treasures, Caddo has been rather routinely abused, first by that steamboat traffic in the 19th century, then again, beginning in 1910, when prospectors built the country’s first “over-water” oil rigs to tap what was hoped to be infinite reserves in East Texas.

In the last 20 years, Caddo has been assaulted by more-contemporary culprits, including mercury and other pollutants from past industrial excesses. So far, with the aid of several environmental organizations, the lake’s defenders have kept much of the vicissitudes of the 21st century at bay. It is one of 26 areas in the country designated for protection by the Ramsar Convention, a 1970s treaty that seeks to protect wetlands by asking participating nations to designate, put to “best” use and, if necessary, completely protect certain areas.
What was this article about?

This article recounts the personal experiences and impressions of visitors to Caddo Lake, located on the Texas-Louisiana border. The lake, which is the only naturally formed lake in Texas, offers activities such as fishing, canoeing, and bird watching, while also giving the environment a unique character with its wetlands and bald cypress trees. The piece delves into the local legends and tales surrounding Caddo Lake, which is shrouded in an atmosphere of supernatural and eerie beauty.

The history of Caddo Lake dates back to the early 1600s when the Caddo Indians settled in the area. It is believed to have been formed either by an earthquake and flooding, or as a result of the Great Raft, a huge log jam in the Red River. The lake played a role in the booming trade industry in the region during the 19th century, as steamships navigated its waters to reach the port town of Jefferson.

Caddo Lake has faced ecological challenges throughout its history, including pollution from the steamboat traffic during the 19th century and the construction of over-water oil rigs in the early 1900s. In recent times, efforts have been made to protect the lake from further environmental damage, with the Ramsar Convention designating it as one of 26 areas in the United States for protection and preservation.

Q: Q: Where do plankton, nekton, and benthos live?   A:

Choose your answer from: (I) arctic (II) deserts (III) forests (IV) in the oceans
A:

Plankton, nekton, and benthos are all terms used to describe various types of organisms that live primarily within aquatic environments. These organisms are categorized based on their ability to move and their preferred habitat within the water. To answer your question, we will look at each of the four options provided and identify which one corresponds to these three groups of organisms.

I) Arctic
While the Arctic is a region characterized by freezing temperatures, ice, and snow, it does contain aquatic environments such as the Arctic Ocean, where some plankton, nekton, and benthos can be found. However, the Arctic environment is not exclusive to these organisms, nor is it their primary habitat.

II) Deserts
Deserts are characterized by their arid and dry conditions, making them unsuitable habitats for plankton, nekton, and benthos. While some desert aquatic systems, such as oases or ephemeral rivers, may support limited aquatic life, this option would not be considered their primary habitat.

III) Forests
Although forests may contain bodies of water like lakes, rivers, and streams, these habitats alone would not be a primary location for plankton, nekton, and benthos. The majority of organisms found in forest ecosystems consist of terrestrial plants and animals rather than aquatic organisms.

IV) In the oceans
This is the correct answer. Plankton, nekton, and benthos predominantly reside in oceanic environments. Plankton refers to aquatic organisms that drift passively with water currents. They can be divided into two main groups: phytoplankton (photosynthesizing microscopic plants) and zooplankton (tiny animals that feed on phytoplankton or other zooplankton). Nekton includes more mobile and active aquatic creatures such as fish, squid, and marine mammals that can swim against currents or move independently. Benthos refers to organisms that live on, in, or near the ocean floor, such as crabs, clams, sea anemones, and various types of worms.

In summary, plankton, nekton, and benthos primarily live in oceanic environments, making option (IV) the correct choice. Although some of these organisms can be found in other aquatic environments or ecosystems, the ocean is the primary habitat best suited for their survival and growth.

Q:Build a movie plot around this: What pulls Walker into the crater? the aliens
A:

Title: The Crater's Call

Genre: Sci-Fi/ Adventure/ Mystery

Plot:

Act 1:

Walker Daniels, a bright and skilled exobiologist in his mid-30s, is on a research expedition for NASA in Central Africa, where a newly discovered massive crater has caught the attention of the scientific community. As part of a diverse team of scientists, Walker takes the responsibility to study the possibility of extraterrestrial life in this unique geological formation. Little do they know the truth about the crater.

Walker is a dedicated scientist with a curious mind, but he also has a deep personal connection to the search for extraterrestrial life. His mother, who was his source of inspiration, disappeared while working on a classified government project on extraterrestrial signals. Walker has since been obsessively searching for any clues regarding her disappearance.

As the team settles into their research camp near the crater, they are met by Sayida Awad, a local anthropologist, who has knowledge about the crater's history and the unexplained phenomena that have been reported by the natives. Strange stories of missing people and mysterious lights over the crater fascinate and intrigue the team, but they initially dismiss them as superstitions and folklore.

Act 2:

Walker and the team descend into the depths of the crater equipped with the latest scientific equipment. The deeper the team goes, the stranger things become. Unexplainable magnetic fields, bizarre radio signals, and peculiar luminescent organisms are some of the findings they come across.

One night, while examining the anomalous radio signals, Walker starts to receive cryptic messages in his mother's voice, urging him to follow the trail and venture deeper into the crate. The messages leave Walker both distraught and determined. He hides this information from the rest of the team, fearing they may think he is losing his sanity.

Despite being warned by Sayida and experiencing the strange occurrences plaguing the team members, Walker becomes obsessed by the quest. His determination to find the truth behind his mother's disappearance forms a fierce internal conflict between his scientific perspective and the unfathomable unknown.

Act 3:

As the team prepares to leave the crater due to lack of significant findings and continuous inexplicable occurrences, Walker secretly decides to stay behind and investigate further. As night falls, he feels an irresistible pull towards the crater's center. He can no longer resist the calling, and heads towards the source.

On reaching the heart of the crater, Walker discovers a concealed and technologically advanced underground alien base. He gathers up his courage and walks inside, trying to unravel the alien technology and understanding their mission. Walker comes across a room where he finds his mother in a state of suspended animation. He realizes that she has been kept alive by the aliens for her knowledge on extraterrestrial signals.

The aliens, who have been observing Walker all this time, finally reveal themselves. They explain that they abducted his mother to utilize her expertise to communicate with their home planet. They have been stranded on Earth after their spaceship crash-landed, forming the massive crater. Realizing the sincerity in Walker's quest, the aliens allow his mother to return with him, as they have managed to make contact with their home planet.

Intercept Pharma's Shares Are Spiking After a Key FDA Panel Backing
Intercept Pharmaceuticals’ icpt stock rose as much as 11% in after-hours trading on Thursday after an FDA advisory committee unanimously recommended approval of a rare liver disease treatment developed by the company. But shares slumped nearly 7% in early trading Friday after Morgan Stanley downgraded the stock. Although Intercept’s shares have surged more than 30% in the past month in anticipation of the FDA ruling, Morgan Stanley analysts concluded that the liver drug may not fulfill its market potential due to safety concerns in some patients with more advanced versions of the disease.

Intercept is working on a treatment for primary biliary cirrhosis, or PBC, a chronic liver ailment that can eventually degrade bile ducts to the point of permanent scarring. The disease disproportionately affects women.

Thursday’s advisory panel voted 17-0 in favor of approving the drug despite some previously stated safety concerns. The FDA isn’t required to follow independent panel recommendations, but usually does, making the therapy’s approval all but assured.

The agency will decide whether or not to approve the treatment by May 29.

While an approval for PBC could bring in millions in sales, some analysts have also predicted that number could balloon into the billions if the Intercept drug can win an FDA approval for the more common nonalcoholic steatohepatitis, or NASH, a condition in which fatty deposits in the liver cause inflammation.
What is a short summary of the above article?

Intercept Pharmaceuticals' stock rose by 11% after an FDA advisory committee unanimously recommended approval for their rare liver disease treatment. The treatment, intended for primary biliary cirrhosis (PBC), could bring millions in sales with approval. The FDA will make its final decision by May 29th. Although there are concerns about the drug's safety when used for more advanced versions of the disease, the approval could lead to a significant market potential, particularly if the drug can obtain FDA approval for treating nonalcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH).

Please briefly summarize this news article:

How the Economic Recovery Is Leaving Many Behind

By Rana Foroohar Monday, Aug. 12, 2013

Job seekers wait in line to meet with a recruiter during the East Bay's HIREvent on July 17, 2013 in Emeryville, Calif.

For some time now, the U.S. has been the prettiest house on what is an ugly economic block. Look around: China and many of the other major emerging markets are slowing down, and Europe is in recession. The U.S., on the other hand, finally seems to be moving beyond 2% annual economic growth toward a more robust recovery. The big question is whether it will be a recovery for everyone.

The answer so far seems to be a definitive no. The percentage of Americans in the labor force, arguably the most important measure of economic health, dropped to a 34-year low in May. That's as bad as it's been since women entered the labor force en masse in the 1980s, and it speaks to the fact that this recovery is weaker and has taken longer than rebounds of the past. We're 30 months on from when GDP returned to prerecession levels, but even at June's high level of job creation (195,000) it will take 15 months more to replace the jobs that were lost. While the unemployment figure is slowly ticking downward from the crisis' peak of 10%, it remains in double digits for certain groups, such as young people without college degrees and African Americans. Meanwhile, economic inequality has increased, since minorities were disproportionately hit in the housing crisis, and wages have stayed flat. It's hard to envision an inclusive recovery when a majority of Americans simply haven't got more money in their pockets.

That puts the American Dream at risk. "If young Americans are to live better than their parents did, we have to expand employment, make the workforce more competitive and accelerate productivity growth," which is strongly correlated with rising wages, says Susan Lund, a principal at the McKinsey Global Institute, which just released a report titled "Game Changers" on how to build a more robust recovery in the U.S. One key challenge will be to create more sustainable demand in an economy that is 70% driven by consumer spending. Yes, Americans have gone a long way toward repairing their balance sheets since 2008. Fewer of us have debt than we did back then. But those who do, including vulnerable groups like students and seniors, have 40% more debt than they did before the financial crisis, according to the Census Bureau. And consumer spending is still spotty. While overall retail sales have grown over the past year, June numbers came in far weaker than expected. Department-store sales were down for the fifth month in a row, and restaurant spending was sharply down as consumers once again ate in to save a dollar. People even bought fewer electronic gadgets for a third month running. The latest GDP figure--1.7%--shows we are still struggling to stay in a 2% economy. "To say we're doing better than Europe is meager consolation," says Lund. "We need to do much better."

Of course, the economy is cyclical, and one bad quarter won't derail a recovery. What you want to see is sustained job growth, and the most recent new-hire numbers were stellar. The question is whether they were a blip or a real directional shift from what we've seen over the past several years. Even before the financial crisis, there were signs that the U.S. jobs engine was sputtering. Job creation from 2000 to 2007 was far weaker than in decades past. This is in part because small and new companies, which do much of the real hiring in the U.S., still have problems getting bank loans. Indeed, Urban Institute senior fellow Gene Steuerle believes that one key reason for the declining number of jobs being created by new businesses is that entrepreneurs have exhausted their personal savings, which most of them use in lieu of loans to fund investment. Banks, which claim they are trying to meet higher capital requirements, won't step in to help.

This underscores the fact that five years on from the crisis, finance is still disconnected from the real economy. Sure, banks are recording record profits, but numerous studies show that as finance as a percentage of the economy grows, business creation actually tends to stall. A banking recovery isn't enough to turn the economy around--we need manufacturing and services to grow too. Yes, a manufacturing renaissance is under way. But it isn't your grandfather's manufacturing. Well-paid union jobs have given way to cheaper labor and to robots that do work that people used to do, which is partly why wages have remained flat.

That's just another reason that too many Americans won't feel the buoyancy of this divided rebound for many years to come.

The US economic recovery appears to be leaving many people behind, with the percentage of Americans in the labor force dropping to a 34-year low in May 2013. The recovery looks weaker and longer than previous rebounds, with unemployment still high for some groups and economic inequality on the rise. A key challenge for an inclusive recovery is creating sustainable demand in a consumer-driven economy. Banks are recording record profits, but studies show that when finance is a large part of the economy, business creation can stall. Although a manufacturing renaissance is happening, well-paid union jobs have been replaced by cheaper labor and automation, contributing to stagnant wages.

Whitehead has had some influence on philosophy of business administration and organizational theory. This has led in part to a focus on identifying and investigating the effect of temporal events (as opposed to static things) within organizations through an “organization studies” discourse that accommodates a variety of 'weak' and 'strong' process perspectives from a number of philosophers. One of the leading figures having an explicitly Whiteheadian and panexperientialist stance towards management is Mark Dibben, who works in what he calls "applied process thought" to articulate a philosophy of management and business administration as part of a wider examination of the social sciences through the lens of process metaphysics. For Dibben, this allows "a comprehensive exploration of life as perpetually active experiencing, as opposed to occasional – and thoroughly passive – happening." Dibben has published two books on applied process thought, Applied Process Thought I: Initial Explorations in Theory and Research (2008), and Applied Process Thought II: Following a Trail Ablaze (2009), as well as other papers in this vein in the fields of philosophy of management and business ethics.

Mark Dibben refers to his philosophy on management and business administration as "applied process thought." He uses this philosophy to articulate a view of management and business administration as part of a wider examination of the social sciences through the lens of process metaphysics. This philosophy aligns with the ideas of philosopher Whitehead, of whom Dibben takes an explicitly Whiteheadian and panexperientialist stance in his applied process thought. Article:A directive to shut the Dadaab camp and forcibly repatriate about 260,000 Somali refugees living there was issued last year.
The deadline for its closure had been extended until May, but a high court judge ruled the decision was tantamount to an act of group persecution.
The government says it will appeal against the ruling on security grounds.
It said that attacks on its soil by the Somalia-based al-Shabab group had been planned in the camp.
"The camp had lost its humanitarian nature and had become a haven for terrorism and other illegal activities," Kenyan government spokesman Eric Kiraithe said.
"The lives of Kenyans matter. Our interest in this case, and in the closure of Dadaab refugee camp, remains to protect the lives of Kenyans," he added.
Dadaab was set up in 1991 to house families fleeing conflict in Somalia, and some people have been living there for more than 20 years.
The Kenya National Commission on Human Rights and lobby group Kituo Cha Sheria challenged the decision in court, saying it was discriminatory and contrary to international law.
"The government's decision specifically targeting Somali refugees is an act of group persecution, illegal discriminatory and therefore unconstitutional," Judge John Mativo said.
The orders are excessive, arbitrary and disproportionate, he said.
The BBC's Nancy Kacungira in the capital, Nairobi, says the ruling means the government has to start putting in place mechanisms that will restore regular operations at the camp even though it will launch an appeal.
Kenya's refugee department had already been shut, which meant that new-born babies were not being registered as refugees, Said Abuka, a community leader and refugee in Nairobi, told the Associated Press news agency.
Amnesty International welcomed the ruling, saying it affirmed Kenya's legal obligation to protect people who seek safety from harm and persecution.
"Today is a historic day for more than a quarter of a million refugees who were at risk of being forcefully returned to Somalia, where they would have been at serious risk of human rights abuses," the rights group's Muthoni Wanyeki said in a statement.
Mr Kiraithe said since the closure of Dadaab was announced, more than 51,000 Somalis had voluntarily returned to Somalia.
"We are encouraged that Somalia continues to stabilise politically more so with the election of a new president," he added.
Somalia's 'Mr Cheese' president has a lot on his plate
Somalia's UN-backed government, with the help of a 22,000-strong African Union force, is battling Islamist group al-Shabab to regain control of the country.
But it has only managed to secure major towns.
The militants, who impose a strict version of Islam in the areas they control, still mount deadly bombings and have carried out a string of attacks in neighbouring Kenya.
Kenya is currently building a fence along its 700km (435-mile) border with Somalia, but has completed only 4km so far.

A summary of the above article is?

Last year, a directive was issued to shut the Dadaab camp and forcibly repatriate around 260,000 Somali refugees in the camp. The deadline for the closure was extended until May, but a high court judge decided the decision to close the camp was an act of group persecution. The Kenyan government argues that the camp has become a haven for terrorism and illegal activities, and has planned an appeal against the ruling. Several human rights groups, however, view the ruling as significant for the protection of those fleeing war and persecution.

I believe that our comrades should make such statistical survey.

It seems like the original text meant to convey the following message: "I believe that our comrades should make such a statistical survey." Here, the emphasis is on the significance of developing statistical surveys among a group of people working together, likely in a professional or academic context. The statement underscores the value of collecting and analyzing data to further understand a subject or to make more informed decisions.

To elaborate more on why statistical surveys are essential, consider the following points:

1. Informed Decision-Making: Statistical surveys provide essential data that can be used to make informed decisions. By understanding the distribution, trends, and patterns within the data, colleagues can develop new strategies, improve existing practices, and even identify any potential problems that may need to be addressed urgently.

2. Encouraging Collaboration: Conducting a statistical survey requires teamwork and coordination among the group members. This process fosters cooperation and collaboration, as members must rely on each other's skills, knowledge, and capabilities to gather accurate data and insights.

3. Enhanced Understanding: Statistical surveys can reveal surprising or non-intuitive insights about the subject being studied. This new information can help to refine existing knowledge, challenge assumptions, or perhaps aid in generating novel ideas or approaches.

4. Benchmarking Progress: Once the initial survey is completed, the data can serve as a benchmark for future surveys, allowing the team to track progress over time. Monitoring changes and progress is crucial in evaluating the effectiveness of implemented strategies or pinpointing any areas that need improvement.

5. Efficient Allocation of Resources: The data obtained from statistical surveys can help identify where resources should be better allocated, ensuring that time, personnel, and funding are directed towards the most promising projects or tasks.

Overall, conducting a statistical survey can prove to be highly beneficial, promoting evidence-based decision-making, teamwork, and better understanding among team members.

Write an article based on this "Plug the DSL modem into your computer. Plug the DSL modem into the wall cable socket. Plug in the DSL modem's power cord. Log in to the modem's administrative screen. Enter your DSL account username and password. Save your settings. Test your internet connection."

Article:

Title: A Step-by-Step Guide to Set Up and Optimize Your DSL Modem

Introduction

Digital Subscriber Line (DSL) technology provides broadband internet services through existing phone lines, making it a popular and cost-effective option for many homes and businesses. By using a DSL modem, you can access the internet through your phone line without interrupting your landline connection. In this article, we will walk you through the process of setting up and optimizing your DSL modem, ensuring a smooth and efficient internet experience with minimal downtime.

1. Choose the right DSL modem

Before you can set up your DSL modem, make sure you have the appropriate hardware for your network. You can either purchase a standalone modem or a modem-router combo, depending on your needs and preferences. A modem-router combo unit provides the functionality of both a modem and a router in one device, simplifying the setup process and reducing clutter.

2. Plug the DSL modem into your computer

Once you have your DSL modem, it's time to connect it to your computer. Locate the Ethernet port at the back of your modem and connect an Ethernet cable to it. Plug the other end of the cable into the Ethernet port on your computer. This will establish a direct line of communication between your modem and computer, allowing you to configure the modem settings and access the internet through the device.

3. Plug the DSL modem into the wall cable socket

Next, locate the telephone jack in your wall and connect the DSL modem to it using a DSL cable or a phone cord provided with your modem. Make sure that the cable you use is compatible with DSL service, as a regular telephone cable may not work. This connection will enable the transmission of DSL signals from your telecommunication provider to your modem.

4. Plug in the DSL modem's power cord

Connect the power adapter provided with your modem to the power port on the device. Plug the other end into an electrical outlet. Turn the modem on by pressing the power button, if applicable. Wait for the modem to initialize and establish a connection with your DSL provider. This process may take a few minutes, and you should see various indicator lights on the modem light up as the connection is established.

5. Log in to the modem's administrative screen

Once your modem is connected and powered on, you need to access its administrative interface to configure the settings. Open a web browser on your computer and enter the modem's default IP address in the address bar. Common IP addresses for modems include 192.168.1.1, 192.168.0.1, and 10.0.0.1. You can find your specific modem's IP address in the user manual or on a label attached to the device.

6. Enter your DSL account username and password

After navigating to the modem's IP address, you will be prompted to enter a username and password. By default, most modems are configured with "admin" as the username and "password" as the password. If these credentials don't work, consult your modem's manual or contact your ISP for assistance. Once logged in, locate the internet or WAN settings page.

Enter the DSL account username and password provided by your internet service provider (ISP). This information is crucial for establishing a connection to the internet, so make sure you enter the details correctly. If you are unsure about your DSL account credentials, contact your ISP for assistance.

7. Save your settings

After entering your DSL account information, review your modem settings, and apply any necessary changes. Save the updated settings and your modem will apply the new configuration. You may need to restart the modem for the changes to take effect.

8. Test your internet connection

Once your modem settings have been updated and the device has restarted, check your internet connection by opening a web browser and visiting a website. If you can access the site without any issues, your DSL modem setup is complete.

9. Optimize your modem settings (optional)

To improve the performance of your DSL connection, you may want to optimize your modem settings. Consult your modem's user manual for recommended settings to ensure the best possible internet experience.

Conclusion

Setting up a DSL modem is relatively simple, even for those without technical expertise. By carefully following these steps and consulting your modem's user manual, you can enjoy a stable and fast internet connection with minimal downtime. Additionally, optimizing your modem settings can help maximize your internet performance and provide a hassle-free online experience.

Please answer the following question: This is a correct answer to the following question about H.264/MPEG-4 AVC. Yes or no? Answer: H.264/MPEG-4 AVC is a block-oriented motion-compensation -based codec standard developed by the ITU-T Video Coding Experts Group (VCEG) together with the ISO/IEC JTC1 Moving Picture Experts Group (MPEG). Question: what is mpeg4  avc or sp/asp
A:

Yes, your answer about H.264/MPEG-4 AVC being a block-oriented motion-compensation-based codec standard developed by the ITU-T Video Coding Experts Group (VCEG) together with the ISO/IEC JTC1 Moving Picture Experts Group (MPEG) is correct. 

Now, let me address the question you asked: "what is MPEG-4 AVC or SP/ASP?"

MPEG-4 AVC, also known as H.264, is a video compression standard that provides good video quality at low bit rates. It is widely used in various applications, including video streaming, videoconferencing, and Blu-ray discs. The standard was jointly developed by the ITU-T Video Coding Experts Group (VCEG) and the ISO/IEC Moving Picture Experts Group (MPEG), as you correctly mentioned.

SP (Simple Profile) and ASP (Advanced Simple Profile) are two profiles under the MPEG-4 Part 2 video compression standard, which is also known as MPEG-4 Visual. These profiles define different sets of encoding tools and compression capabilities to cater to different video applications and devices.

Simple Profile (SP) is designed for low-complexity video encoding. It supports mainly progressive video with resolutions up to 352x288 pixels and is suitable for applications like videoconferencing and streaming on the internet. Advanced Simple Profile (ASP), on the other hand, includes more advanced encoding tools and supports interlaced as well as progressive video. This profile is aimed at providing better compression efficiency and video quality for a wide range of applications, including high-quality video streaming and broadcasting.

In summary, MPEG-4 AVC (H.264) is a highly efficient video compression standard with widespread adoption, while MPEG-4 SP/ASP are profiles under the MPEG-4 Part 2 standard, aimed at different video encoding complexity levels and various applications.
